channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Federal debt exceeds $39 trillion for the 1st time. Why is this milestone significant?
item.description: The federal debt passed an uncomfortable milestone this year, outgrowing the entire U.S. economy. That poses risks, but policymakers show little sign of addressing it.
item.content:encoded: The federal debt passed an uncomfortable milestone this year, outgrowing the entire U.S. economy. That poses risks, but policymakers show little sign of addressing it.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Iran war shakes Global economy as energy costs surge and recession fears grow
item.description: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.content:encoded: From energy markets to everyday prices, the fallout from the Iran war is reshaping the global economy.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: The effects of the Iran war on Americans' wallet
item.description: Gas prices just hit a 4-year record, and the price of jet fuel has doubled. NPR speaks to industry executives and energy experts to explain the areas in which Americans are feeling the pinch.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices just hit a 4-year record, and the price of jet fuel has doubled. NPR speaks to industry executives and energy experts to explain the areas in which Americans are feeling the pinch.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: The Iran war has caused oil and gas prices to skyrocket. How it's affecting consumers
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks business news analyst Jill Schlesinger how high oil and gas prices from the Iran war affect consumer budgets.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks business news analyst Jill Schlesinger how high oil and gas prices from the Iran war affect consumer budgets.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: The economy is growing – but soaring energy prices could put a damper on that
item.description: The U.S. economy grew at an annual pace of 2% in the first three months of the year. That's an improvement from the previous quarter. But soaring energy prices could put a damper on growth.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. economy grew at an annual pace of 2% in the first three months of the year. That's an improvement from the previous quarter. But soaring energy prices could put a damper on growth.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Gas prices hit highest point since Iran war began
item.description: Gas prices are at their highest since the war with Iran began.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices are at their highest since the war with Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: The U.S. economy shows resilience despite the war with Iran
item.description: The U.S. economy revved up in the first three months of the year, despite a spike in energy prices caused by the war with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. economy revved up in the first three months of the year, despite a spike in energy prices caused by the war with Iran.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Spirit Airlines tried to be the Dollar General of the skies. Then the big airlines beat it at its own game
item.description: Spirit Airlines helped pioneer ultra-cheap flying and soared. Then legacy airlines copied them, outmaneuvered them with loyalty programs, and the economy turned against their core customers.
item.content:encoded:
Spirit Airlines helped pioneer ultra-cheap flying and soared. Then legacy airlines copied them, outmaneuvered them with loyalty programs, and the economy turned against their core customers.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Jerome Powell to remain on Fed's board after stepping down as chair
item.description: The Federal Reserve voted to hold interest rates steady, at what's likely Jerome Powell's last meeting as Fed chair. Kevin Warsh looks set to replace Powell next month.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Reserve voted to hold interest rates steady, at what's likely Jerome Powell's last meeting as Fed chair. Kevin Warsh looks set to replace Powell next month.

(Image credit: Sophie Park)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Why Sen. Warren voted no for Kevin Warsh as Fed chairman
item.description: Democratic Sen. Elizabeth Warren discusses the future of the Federal Reserve and tells NPR's Steve Inskeep why she plans to vote no on Kevin Warsh's nomination as Fed chair.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic Sen. Elizabeth Warren discusses the future of the Federal Reserve and tells NPR's Steve Inskeep why she plans to vote no on Kevin Warsh's nomination as Fed chair.

(Image credit: Kent Nishimura)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
